Subjects Covered: Reading, writing, spelling, language arts, mathematics, history, geography, science, health, physical education, music, visual arts
Curriculum Materials: Montessori-inspired materials, picture books, play money, art supplies, musical instruments
Learning Objectives: Develop fine and gross motor skills; recognize numbers 1–10, basic letters, primary colors; build social skills; foster curiosity; understand community roles
Sample Activities: Counting Game: Sort counters into trays labeled 1–5; Story Time: Read The Opposite Zoo; Color Sorting: Match colored sprinkles; Art Project: Snowman handprints; Music & Movement: Dance with scarves
Assessments: Observational checklists for motor skills, social interactions, recognition; parent feedback (Week 8); portfolio of art projects
Extracurricular Opportunities: Robotics program for siblings in grades 1–12

Subjects Covered: Reading, writing, spelling, language arts, mathematics, history, geography, science, health, physical education, music, visual arts
Curriculum Materials: Literacy Essentials, Primary Phonics, All About Spelling, Dimensions Math, My PALS Science, IEW, Tuttle Twins, Our America
Learning Objectives: Master phonograms and spelling rules; perform addition/subtraction within 100; understand plant/animal life cycles, weather patterns; apply financial literacy; develop narrative writing
Sample Activities: Literacy Station: Decode Mac and Tab, practice CK rule; Math Station: Solve addition with number bonds; Science Experiment: Cloud in a jar; History Lesson: Read Our America; Creative Writing: Write daily news
Assessments: Weekly phonogram/spelling quizzes; math workbook assessments (Weeks 9–10); project evaluations; progress reports (Week 8); portfolio
